back_translation: This saying is trustworthy, namely: if anyone aspires to the position of overseeing-caretaker, he desires a good work.

Therefore, the overseeing-caretaker must be a person without fault, a husband of only one wife, knowing-his-own-measure, possessing mindfulness-and-awareness, dignified and respectable, hospitable to guests, and skilled in teaching.
Not addicted to liquor, not using violence, but polite and gentle, not fond of quarreling, not greedy for money.
Managing his own household well, with children who are under discipline with full respect.
For if anyone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of God's Church?
Must not be one who has just newly believed, lest he become arrogant and fall into the same judgment as the Devil.
Moreover, he must also be spoken of well by outsiders, lest he fall into shame and the snare of the Devil.
Likewise, deacons must be worthy of respect, not two-tongued, not addicted to liquor, not greedy for shameful profit.
Holding to the profound mystery of the faith with a pure conscience.
Let these people be tested first, and then if they are without fault, let them serve in the position of deacon.
Women likewise must be worthy of respect, not persons fond of slandering, temperate, and faithful in all matters.
Deacons must be husbands of only one wife, managing their children and their own households well.
For those who have carried out the duty of deacon well will obtain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
I write these things to you, while hoping to come to you soon.
But if I am delayed, you will know how one should conduct oneself in the household of God, which is the Church of the living God, being the pillar and foundation of the truth.

And according to common confession, the mystery of the way of God is exceedingly great, namely: He appeared in the flesh, was vindicated by the Spirit, appeared to the angels, was proclaimed among the nations, was believed on in the world, was taken up into glory.
